# Workbench

> Workbench is an open-source, MIT-licensed BullMQ dashboard. It ships as a
> native macOS desktop app for local-first queue inspection, and as a
> drop-in server-mounted dashboard with first-party adapters for thirteen
> Node.js frameworks. Workbench is the modern alternative to bull-board:
> wider framework coverage, FlowProducer DAG visualisation, error triage
> grouped by exception class, scheduler timeline for cron / delayed jobs,
> and a keyboard-driven UI.

## Quick facts

- **Project**: Workbench — the missing dashboard for BullMQ
- **License**: MIT (free, open source, no telemetry)
- **Source**: https://github.com/pontusab/workbench
- **Install (any supported framework)**: `npx @getworkbench/cli init`
- **Desktop app**: macOS 11+, Apple-signed, auto-updates from signed releases
- **Author**: Pontus Abrahamsson — https://x.com/pontusab

## Supported frameworks (first-party adapters)

Each adapter is a `@getworkbench/<framework>` package with a smoke-tested
example app in the monorepo.

- Hono — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/hono
- Elysia — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/elysia
- Express — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/express
- Fastify — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/fastify
- NestJS — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/nestjs
- AdonisJS — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/adonis
- Next.js — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/next
- TanStack Start — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/tanstack-start
- Koa — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/koa
- Astro — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/astro
- Nuxt — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/nuxt
- Bun (Bun.serve) — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/bun
- h3 — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/h3

## AI agent integrations (MCP)

MCP server that lets Cursor, Claude Desktop, Zed, Continue.dev and any
other MCP-aware agent inspect, debug, and operate BullMQ queues through a
running Workbench dashboard. The MCP is a thin local proxy: it speaks
JSON-RPC over stdio to your editor and HTTP to the dashboard, inheriting
the dashboard's existing Basic Auth and `readonly` flag (no second source
of truth for permissions). Ships 18 tools split across two intents —
inspect (overview, queues, runs, jobs, metrics, schedulers, flows,
search) and operate (retry, remove, promote, pause/resume queue, run
scheduler now, enqueue, clean, bulk retry/delete).

- @getworkbench/mcp — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@getworkbench/mcp

- Bun: https://getworkbench.dev/blog/bullmq-dashboard-for-bun
- h3: https://getworkbench.dev/blog/bullmq-dashboard-for-h3
- MCP (Cursor/Claude/Zed/Continue): https://getworkbench.dev/blog/bullmq-mcp-server

## What Workbench includes

- Live counters, p50/p95 latency, and throughput sparklines per queue.
- Virtualised runs table with status filters, full payloads, keyboard retry.
- FlowProducer DAG view for parent / child jobs.
- Scheduler timeline for cron + delayed jobs (pause, resume, edit cron live).
- Error triage grouped by exception class with 24h trend lines.
- Click any stack-trace line in a failed job to jump straight into Cursor or
  VS Code.
- Same UI in the desktop app and in the embedded server dashboard.

## Pricing

Workbench is fully open source under the MIT license. There is no paid tier,
no telemetry, and no account required. Sponsor the project at
https://github.com/sponsors/pontusab if you want to support development.
